pi_items.source: Verified via task.json description, setup_mysql_workbench.sh (which installs sakila), and export_result.sh script targeting the 'sakila' database.
Verified via task.json description and export_result.sh which explicitly checks for this trigger name.
Verified via task.json description and export_result.sh 'EVENT_OBJECT_TABLE=customer'.
Verified via setup_task.sh which explicitly creates the 'customer_audit_log' table.
Verified via task.json description and export_result.sh which explicitly checks for this procedure name.
Verified via setup_task.sh which explicitly creates the 'customer_loyalty' table.
Verified via task.json description and export_result.sh which uses this file path for validation.
Verified by downloading the official Sakila db sql dump and extracting exactly 599 inserted rows for the 'customer' table.
Verified via task.json description text stating 'Bronze' tier applies to 0-5 rentals.
Verified via task.json description text stating 'Silver' tier applies to 6-20 rentals.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the official MySQL Sakila sample database. The 'customer' table contains exactly 599 authentic records. The task requires building the 'tr_customer_audit' trigger to populate 'customer_audit_log', and the 'sp_calculate_loyalty_tiers' stored procedure to group the 599 customers into 'Bronze' (<=5 rentals) and 'Silver' (<=20 rentals) tiers in the 'customer_loyalty' table. The expected output file path for the loyalty evaluation is '/home/ga/Documents/exports/customer_loyalty.csv'. All metadata claims align with actual environment scripts, the task description, and the native structure of the Sakila database.
